name: Pineal Gland
location: Postero-ventral to the thalamus in the epithalamus between the two cerebral hemispheres.
description: A small pinecone-shaped endocrine structure that synthesizes melatonin from serotonin in response to darkness, regulating sleep-wake cycles and circadian body rhythms.
clinicalImportance: Pineal calcification ('brain sand') serves as an anatomical midline radiological landmark on skull X-rays and CT scans.
researchNotes: René Descartes historically declared the pineal gland to be the 'principal seat of the soul' where mind and body interact.
interestingFacts: Contains photoreceptive evolutionary heritage, often dubbed the 'Third Eye'.
